level_signals: Comp range $175K-$265K base, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time spent on billing, platform, or infrastructure.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time spent on billing, platform, or infrastructure

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building billing, ideally in a SaaS, enterprise software, or AI platform environment.
Strong understanding of the billing stack, including event capture, aggregation, invoicing, revenue recognition, accounting handoffs, and operational controls.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to balance customer needs, commercial priorities, finance requirements, legal considerations, and engineering tradeoffs.
A systems-thinking approach to product development, with the ability to design architectures and workflows that are reliable, transparent, auditable, and adaptable.

scope.surface: Harvey's billing infrastructure — event capture, invoicing, reporting, account-level transparency, and commercial platform for enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise customer trust and payment reliability
Scalability of billing infrastructure as pricing/packaging evolves
core_responsibilities: Drive the vision, strategy, and execution for Harvey's billing infrastructure.
Partner with engineering and design to build billing capabilities that are accurate, auditable, flexible, and intuitive for enterprise customers.
Collaborate with go-to-market, finance, legal, sales, and customer success teams to support complex enterprise contracts.
Shape the customer-facing billing experience, including invoices, reporting, and account-level transparency, while ensuring the underlying infrastructure can scale.
Help Harvey scale a trusted commercial platform that meets the needs of enterprise customers, internal operators, and financial reporting stakeholders.

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (billing infrastructure)
Cross-functional scope described in detail (engineering, design, GTM, finance, legal, sales, CS)
Company has strong traction: 1500+ customers in 60+ countries with stated product-market fit
red_flags: Location not specified anywhere in the JD — work arrangement (remote/hybrid/onsite) is unclear
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not mentioned
Wide comp band ($90K spread) may indicate significant location-based pay differential or level ambiguity
No nice-to-haves section — could reflect high bar with little flexibility or an incomplete JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no mention of direct reports)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer references)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Depending on your location' privacy notice language and wide comp band)
ambiguities: Location and work arrangement are entirely unstated in the JD body; 'Depending on your location' appears only in a privacy notice disclaimer and does not confirm remote eligibility or office locations.

'Experience building billing, ideally in a SaaS, enterprise software, or AI platform environment' uses 'ideally' suggesting a preference, but appears in the 'What You Have' (required) section — treated as hard with a note.
No explicit nice-to-haves section; all listed requirements treated as must-haves per their placement in 'What You Have'.
